title: Heatwave to persist across Central, Northwest India; IMD warns of heavy rain in Northeast - ODISHA RAY
full_text:
Heatwave to persist across Central, Northwest India; IMD warns of heavy rain in Northeast
New Delhi(26/05/2026) By Ishani Chhatoraj : Large parts of Central and Northwest India are likely to continue experiencing severe heatwave conditions over the next few days, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) warned on Tuesday, while simultaneously forecasting isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall across Northeast India during the coming week.According to the weather office, maximum temperatures in several regions of Rajasthan, Haryana, Punjab, Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh and adjoining areas are expected to remain significantly above normal, intensifying heat stress conditions.Daytime temperatures in many districts have already crossed the 45-degree Celsius mark, raising concerns over public health, water scarcity and power demand.The IMD stated that dry northwesterly winds and persistent clear skies are contributing to the prolonged heatwave spell over the plains.Residents in affected states have been advised to avoid prolonged exposure to direct sunlight during afternoon hours, stay hydrated and take precautions against heat-related illnesses such as dehydration and heatstroke.Meanwhile, parts of East and Central India are also likely to witness hot and humid weather conditions over the next few days.Meteorologists indicated that night temperatures in urban centres may remain unusually high, offering little relief from the oppressive heat.In contrast, the Northeast region is expected to receive widespread rainfall activity due to strong moisture incursion and favourable atmospheric conditions.The IMD predicted isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall over states including Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram and Tripura during the next six to seven days.Authorities have cautioned that intense rainfall could trigger localised flooding, waterlogging, landslides and disruption of transport services in vulnerable hilly areas.Disaster management agencies and local administrations have been asked to remain alert and prepared for emergency response measures.Weather experts noted that pre-monsoon activity has begun strengthening over eastern and northeastern parts of the country, while northwest and central regions continue to reel under extreme summer conditions.The southwest monsoon is also advancing gradually, bringing changing weather patterns to several parts of the country.The IMD further advised fishermen not to venture into rough sea areas during adverse weather conditions and urged farmers to follow updated agrometeorological advisories issued by local authorities.With contrasting weather systems affecting different parts of the country simultaneously, officials emphasized the need for public caution and preparedness in both heatwave-affected and rain-hit regions.
